ai_strategy_lead.name: Milan Sonka
ai_strategy_lead.title: Director, Iowa Initiative for Artificial Intelligence (IIAI); Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering

documents.title: University of Iowa Artificial Intelligence Strategy and Guiding Principles
University of Iowa Artificial Intelligence Action Plan
AI in the Classroom (Office of the Executive Vice President and Provost)

ai_strategy.summary: The University of Iowa has published a formal AI Strategy and Guiding Principles plus an AI Action Plan aligned with its 2022-2027 Strategic Plan. The strategy is built on five principles: amplify human potential, collaboration and engagement, excellence, digital and information literacy, and responsibility and integrity. An AI Steering Committee and an AI SPARC (Strategic Plan Action and Resource Committee) recommend actions for implementation across teaching, research, patient care, and operations. President Barbara Wilson has publicly framed AI as a tool the entire campus must learn to use and critique, and the university is simultaneously rolling out enterprise AI (Microsoft 365 Copilot, ChatGPT Edu, an Iowa AI Gateway) and building academic programming (an undergraduate AI Certificate launching fall 2026, HawkAI proficiency courses).

key_provisions: AI tools without a UI contract may only be used with data classified as PUBLIC (Low Sensitivity) under the UI Data Classification standard

ChatGPT Edu may be used with Public or University-Internal data; Restricted-Critical data requires consultation with Research Services or ITS-ISPO; ChatGPT Edu is not HIPAA compliant and cannot be used with patient data
Microsoft 365 Copilot and Copilot Chat are the preferred enterprise AI tools for faculty/staff, offered with enterprise data protection and SSO via HawkID
Instructors must set course-level expectations for AI use in the syllabus; Center for Teaching and OTLT publish model language and an AI Assessment Scale
Users (including faculty/staff) are responsible for verifying accuracy and appropriateness of AI outputs before use; outputs must be treated as draft subject to human review
AI-generated code may not be used in institutional IT systems/services unless human-reviewed
ChatGPT Edu purchases require IT Director or designee approval and are provisioned through the UI workspace so data is not used for model training
Clinical AI tools at UI Health Care must be reviewed by the Digital Health and AI-Based Tool Governance committee before deployment
ai_policy.summary: Iowa's policy posture is comprehensive across faculty, students, staff, research, and clinical use. Published ITS, IT Security, OTLT, Center for Teaching, and UI Health Care guidance collectively define data-classification limits, preferred tools (Copilot Chat, Microsoft 365 Copilot, ChatGPT Edu), syllabus-level requirements, and a separate clinical AI governance pathway. The stance is cautious-comprehensive rather than prohibitive: the university pushes people to approved tools with data protection while allowing experimentation within documented guardrails.

bodies.name: AI Steering Committee
bodies.scope: Develops recommendations and action plans implementing the UI AI Strategy; reports into executive leadership

bodies.name: Artificial Intelligence SPARC (Strategic Plan Action and Resource Committee)
bodies.scope: Tied to the 2022-2027 Strategic Plan; pursues AI-related strategic initiatives; 13 members including ITS, Provost, Engineering, Libraries, Graduate College, and Research leadership (e.g., Steve Fleagle, James Blum, Paul Dilley, Carl Follmer, Tyler Bell)

bodies.name: Digital Health and AI-Based Tool Governance Committee (UI Health Care)
bodies.scope: Clinical-facing AI governance; clinicians, IT specialists, genomics expert, and health-system application leads evaluate AI tools on training data, operations, privacy/security, and patient risk before adoption

bodies.name: Iowa Initiative for Artificial Intelligence (IIAI)
bodies.scope: Research-focused interdisciplinary AI initiative founded in 2018 within the College of Engineering; led by Director Milan Sonka; funds pilots, runs HawkAI training, and operates the AI/GPU Development Lab in Seamans Center 4622

bodies.name: AI-Pedagogy faculty team (AAC&U Institute on AI, Pedagogy, and the Curriculum)
bodies.scope: Six-member faculty/staff team led by Stephanie Preschel developing a campus AI-in-teaching framework through the yearlong AAC&U national institute (2025-26)

bodies.name: Accessible Iowa Initiative / Accessibility Task Force
bodies.scope: Cross-campus initiative bringing UI digital content into Title II ADA compliance by the April 2026 deadline; intersects with AI via automated captioning, Anthology Ally in ICON, and Siteimprove scoring

governance_structure.summary: UI operates a layered governance model: an AI Steering Committee and AI SPARC set enterprise strategy, a separate Digital Health and AI-Based Tool Governance committee reviews clinical AI at UI Health Care, and the Iowa Initiative for Artificial Intelligence (IIAI, est. 2018) drives research collaboration. Pedagogy governance runs through the Provost, OTLT, and Center for Teaching in coordination with an AAC&U-institute faculty team.

initiatives.title: University of Iowa AI Strategy and Guiding Principles + Action Plan
initiatives.description: Published institutional AI strategy built on five guiding principles (amplify human potential, collaboration, excellence, digital/information literacy, responsibility/integrity) with a companion Action Plan covering teaching, research, and operations. The AI Steering Committee and AI SPARC are responsible for implementation and tie AI work to the 2022-2027 Strategic Plan.
initiatives.significance: Formal, publicly posted institution-wide AI strategy with active implementation committees puts UI in a minority of R1 publics that have moved beyond ad-hoc guidance.

investment.description: Implementation funded through P3 (President's Priorities Program) awards and internal resources; no consolidated dollar figure disclosed.

partners: Office of the Executive Vice President and Provost
collaborations.description: Strategy is authored jointly across ITS, the Provost, and the College of Engineering.

initiatives.policy_implications: Creates institutional cover for enterprise AI rollouts and syllabus-level expectations.

initiatives.title: Microsoft 365 Copilot and Copilot Chat campus rollout
initiatives.description: Microsoft Copilot Chat (free tier) is available to all UI faculty, staff, and students via HawkID SSO with enterprise data protection; Microsoft 365 Copilot (premium, $276/user/year) is available to faculty/staff for in-app use across Word, Excel, Outlook, and Teams. ITS moved Microsoft 365 Copilot from pilot to full production in 2025 and added Copilot Chat Agents builder.
initiatives.significance: Campus-wide GenAI access with vendor-backed enterprise data protection establishes Microsoft as the default UI AI layer and demonstrates that large R1 publics can scale AI access without a bespoke platform.

investment.description: Microsoft 365 Copilot carries a $276 per-user annual license billed beginning September 1; Copilot Chat is free. Aggregate campus spend not disclosed.

collaborations.description: Enterprise agreement with Microsoft for Copilot services.

tools_and_vendors.name: Microsoft 365 Copilot
tools_and_vendors.role: In-app AI assistant across Word, Excel, Outlook, Teams

tools_and_vendors.name: Copilot Chat
tools_and_vendors.role: Free generative chat with enterprise data protection

tools_and_vendors.name: Copilot Chat Agents
tools_and_vendors.role: Custom task-specific agents built on Copilot

initiatives.policy_implications: Establishes a 'preferred tool' pathway that implicitly discourages use of unmanaged free AI tools for non-public data.

initiatives.title: ChatGPT Edu available to UI faculty and staff
initiatives.description: On December 7, 2025, ITS announced ChatGPT Edu availability for UI faculty and staff, provisioned through a UI workspace with HawkID SSO. Data is isolated and not used for OpenAI model training. Purchases require IT Director approval; use is limited to Public and University-Internal data.
initiatives.significance: Second enterprise GenAI platform alongside Copilot signals a multi-vendor posture and formally licenses OpenAI's flagship product for institutional use.

investment.funding_source: Unit-level budgets via IT Director approval
investment.description: Purchase cost per license not publicly disclosed; billed to requesting units.

collaborations.description: ChatGPT Edu agreement with OpenAI for institutional use.

tools_and_vendors.name: ChatGPT Edu
tools_and_vendors.role: Enterprise GenAI workspace with data isolation and custom GPT creation

initiatives.policy_implications: Explicitly excludes patient data (not HIPAA compliant) and Restricted-Critical data without ITS-ISPO review.

initiatives.title: Iowa AI Gateway (multi-model AI platform)
initiatives.description: Planned 2026 pilot of an 'Iowa AI Gateway,' a multi-model AI tool intended to give campus users brokered access to multiple foundation-model backends through a single UI-managed front door.
initiatives.significance: Early signal of the 'AI gateway' pattern increasingly adopted by R1s to avoid vendor lock-in and to centralize guardrails; one of the first publicly named gateway efforts in the Big Ten.

tools_and_vendors.name: Iowa AI Gateway
tools_and_vendors.role: Multi-model AI access layer for campus

initiatives.policy_implications: Will likely become the enforcement point for data-classification guardrails.

initiatives.title: Undergraduate Certificate in Artificial Intelligence (launching fall 2026)
initiatives.description: Interdisciplinary undergraduate certificate launching in fall 2026 with an introductory AI Fundamentals pilot course in spring 2026. Requires one core course plus four to six electives drawn from 10 funded courses across Computer Science, Philosophy, Political Science, Sociology, History, Art, Journalism, Classics/Religious Studies, Communication Sciences and Disorders, and Languages/Literatures. Supported by a $195,000, three-year P3 award; core course taught by Tyler Bell (ECE) and Ali Hasan (Philosophy).
initiatives.significance: Open-to-all-majors AI certificate deliberately designed around human-centered and ethical applications, not just CS; one of the more curricularly ambitious R1 AI certificates of 2025-26.

investment.funding_source: UI P3 'AI in Undergraduate Education' award (3 years)
investment.description: $195,000 over three years; generated 30+ course proposals with 11 funded (1 core + 10 electives).

partners: College of Liberal Arts and Sciences
collaborations.description: Cross-college faculty consortium (10 Provost AI Fellows)

initiatives.policy_implications: Embeds AI literacy into general-education pathways for non-CS students.

initiatives.title: HawkAI proficiency courses and certificates
initiatives.description: Joint program of IIAI and ITS offering introductory and advanced AI/ML short courses open to any UI student or employee. Learners can take courses individually or in sequences to earn HawkAI Certificates of Proficiency in AI/ML at Levels 1, 2, and 3, funded via P3 and the College of Engineering.
initiatives.significance: Provides the on-ramp for faculty and staff AI literacy outside of formal degree programs and anchors Iowa's response to the sector-wide 'AI literacy for workforce' imperative.

investment.description: Specific dollar amount not disclosed; delivered through IIAI in Seamans Center.

initiatives.title: AAC&U Institute on AI, Pedagogy, and the Curriculum participation
initiatives.description: Selected as one of 175+ institutions in the AAC&U Institute on AI, Pedagogy, and the Curriculum. Six-member UI team (Stephanie Preschel, Allie Brandriet, Justin Cosner, Elizabeth Crawford, Deirdre Egan, Victoria Maloy) is piloting AI-in-rhetoric strategies in spring 2026 and scaling campus-wide in fall 2026.
initiatives.significance: Anchors Iowa into a national faculty-development cohort and gives its teaching-with-AI framework external benchmarking.

collaborations.description: Monthly sessions, mentoring, and workshops with peer teams.

initiatives.policy_implications: Likely to influence syllabus and assessment guidance pushed to all faculty.

initiatives.title: Iowa Initiative for Artificial Intelligence (IIAI) research hub and pilot program
initiatives.description: Founded in 2018 within the College of Engineering, IIAI is a cross-college research and training initiative led by Director Milan Sonka. It runs competitive AI pilot projects (with support from P3, SPARC, ITS, and the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering), data-scientist consulting (Kang Pyo Lee, Yanan Liu), and the AI/GPU Development Lab in Seamans Center 4622. Associated faculty number 80+.
initiatives.significance: One of the earliest university AI initiatives in the U.S. and the backbone of cross-disciplinary AI research on campus, spanning health, engineering, physical sciences, and humanities.

investment.funding_source: College of Engineering + P3 + SPARC + ITS
investment.description: Operating budget and pilot project funding not publicly itemized.

collaborations.description: Executive Committee members span ECE, Computer Science, Management Sciences, Psychiatry, Biochemistry, and ITS.

tools_and_vendors.name: Argon HPC / GPU resources
tools_and_vendors.role: GPU compute platform for IIAI-backed research

initiatives.title: NSF-funded ImagiQ federated-learning platform for medical AI
initiatives.description: University of Iowa-led $1 million NSF Convergence Accelerator phase-one grant (PI Stephen Baek, with Xiaodong Wu and Nick Street) to develop ImagiQ, an asynchronous decentralized federated-learning platform for training medical AI models on globally distributed patient data. Phase-two potential up to $5 million over 24 months.
initiatives.significance: Places UI at the center of a high-profile national effort on federated learning for healthcare AI and demonstrates Iowa's ability to convene elite peers (Stanford, Harvard, Yale, Chicago, Seoul National) and industry (NVIDIA, Lunit, Digital Diagnostics, Imagoworks, inSEER).
initiatives.policy_implications: Advances federated methods that reduce patient-data sharing risk.

initiatives.title: Argon HPC cluster and AI/GPU Development Lab
initiatives.description: The Argon high-performance computing cluster (deployed February 2017) provides ~23,000 CPU cores and 350+ GPU accelerators and is free at the point of use to UI faculty, staff, and students. Engineering Computing Services and IIAI operate an AI/GPU Development Lab in Seamans Center 4622 for AI method development before production runs on Argon.
initiatives.significance: Provides the compute substrate that makes IIAI's pilot program and ImagiQ research possible; one of the larger GPU footprints among Big Ten non-flagship (non-Michigan/Illinois) publics.

investment.description: Free central allocation plus condo-model purchased/rented nodes.

collaborations.description: Joint operations model between ITS and Engineering.

tools_and_vendors.name: NVIDIA GPUs (1080 Ti, Titan V, and successors)

initiatives.title: UI Health Care clinical AI deployments: Evidently and Nabla
initiatives.description: UI Health Care deployed Evidently, an AI chart-review application within Epic that summarizes patient history for providers, and Nabla, an ambient clinical scribing tool that generates draft notes during patient visits. Evidently hit 2,000+ clinician accesses on day one after piloting with 20 providers; Nabla's five-week pilot produced a 4.3/5 clinician rating and reported a 26% reduction in burnout symptoms.
initiatives.significance: Rare example of a university health system moving multiple GenAI tools into full clinician deployment simultaneously, with a disclosed (if informal) outcomes-monitoring approach.

collaborations.description: Vendor partnerships integrated into Epic EHR.

tools_and_vendors.name: Evidently
tools_and_vendors.role: AI chart-review / patient-data summarization inside Epic

initiatives.policy_implications: Reviewed and approved by UI Health Care's Digital Health and AI-Based Tool Governance committee before deployment.

initiatives.title: Operational AI chatbot pilots (HR, OneIT, Enrollment Management)
initiatives.description: ITS is running chatbot pilots in HR, OneIT (central IT), and Enrollment Management. The chatbots are built on Microsoft Copilot Agents and deployed behind HawkID SSO for employees/students.
initiatives.significance: Demonstrates that UI is operationalizing AI for day-to-day administrative self-service rather than only for research or classroom experimentation.
initiatives.policy_implications: Pilots operate under existing Copilot data-protection terms.

initiatives.title: Tippie College of Business AI and analytics program suite
initiatives.description: Tippie operates a cluster of AI-focused academic programs: a graduate Certificate in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning, a Certificate in Artificial Intelligence and Technology Management, an MBA concentration in AI and Technology Management, a Master of Science in Business Analytics (on-campus and part-time/online), and stand-alone courses such as BAIS:6240 Value Creation Using AI and BAIS:6250 Applied Deep Learning. A Tippie AI lab sits alongside an Engineering AI lab on campus.
initiatives.significance: Positions a non-engineering college as an AI talent-development engine, matching the broader sector trend of business schools formalizing AI pathways.

investment.description: Operating investment not broken out publicly.

initiatives.title: Center for Teaching / OTLT syllabus-language and AI Assessment Scale
initiatives.description: The Center for Teaching and Office of Teaching, Learning, and Technology jointly publish syllabus-language templates, assignment examples, and the AI Assessment Scale (a rubric for instructors to decide how much AI use is appropriate in each assignment). Spring 2025 CLAS-specific guidance pushes these resources to liberal-arts instructors.
initiatives.significance: Codifies syllabus-level AI expectations as a normative faculty practice, with UI tooling that other institutions borrow.

partners: Office of Teaching, Learning, and Technology

tools_and_vendors.name: AI Assessment Scale
tools_and_vendors.role: Rubric framework for AI in assignment design

initiatives.policy_implications: Backs up the syllabus-level requirement for AI policy statements.

initiatives.title: Accessible Iowa: ADA Title II digital-accessibility initiative
initiatives.description: Campus-wide initiative launched in 2025 to bring all UI digital content into compliance with the April 24, 2026 ADA Title II deadline. Specific AI-adjacent workstreams include making all UI ICON (Canvas) courses fully accessible via Anthology Ally (which uses AI to generate alt text and automated captions) and targeting a Siteimprove accessibility score of 94 across UI websites.
initiatives.significance: Ties AI-enabled accessibility tooling directly to a compliance deadline, illustrating how R1s are using AI to close equity gaps for disabled students and staff.

collaborations.description: Vendor-backed accessibility scanning and AI remediation.

tools_and_vendors.name: Anthology Ally
tools_and_vendors.role: AI-driven accessibility and alternative-format generation in ICON (Canvas)

initiatives.policy_implications: Moves accessibility from aspiration to enforceable target with a hard deadline.

initiatives.title: IIHR Flood AI and hydroinformatics research
initiatives.description: IIHR-Hydroscience & Engineering operates the Flood AI system (a 24/7 AI-driven flood forecasting platform) initially seeded by a Microsoft AI for Earth grant (2017) and ongoing climate-AI research led by Gabriele Villarini, Wei Zhang, and Ibrahim Demir. The Hydroinformatics Lab maintains online data systems for water quality, flood projects, and well-driller information.
initiatives.significance: Canonical example of applied AI-for-science at Iowa, tying a legacy research strength (hydroscience) to modern machine-learning methods and to public-benefit services for Iowans.

investment.funding_source: Microsoft AI for Earth + federal/state grants

collaborations.description: Industry + state partners for public-benefit research.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Accessible Iowa: The Countdown to Digital Accessibility
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Campus-wide initiative driving ADA Title II compliance by April 24, 2026, with AI-enabled tools (Anthology Ally, Siteimprove) applied across ICON courses and UI websites; target Siteimprove score of 94 across all UI sites.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Universal free Copilot Chat access for students
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: By distributing Microsoft Copilot Chat free to all students via HawkID, UI reduces the 'frontier-model access' equity gap between students who can and can't afford paid GenAI tools.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: HawkAI proficiency courses open to all UI employees and students
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Free or low-cost AI/ML micro-credential track accessible regardless of college or program, lowering the barrier to AI literacy for non-STEM staff and students.

regulatory_context.summary: The State of Iowa has an Enterprise Generative Artificial Intelligence Policy (PY-AI) via the Iowa Department of Management that restricts unapproved free AI tools within state government and requires human review of AI outputs. The Iowa Board of Regents does not (as of April 2026) publish a single system-wide AI policy binding all three Regent universities; each campus has its own. Iowa lawmakers have been active on AI regulation, considering bills on minor protection, health-care AI, and general AI oversight. Iowa State operates the separately chartered Translational AI Research and Education Center (TrAC), and University of Northern Iowa maintains its own AI guidelines — collectively, Iowa's higher-ed AI context is coordinated at the state policy level but decentralized at the system level.

controversies_and_litigation.summary: UI requested closure of six undergraduate majors and a master's in African American World Studies as part of a low-enrollment program review; critics framed the move as an attack on intellectual freedom. While not strictly an AI story, it intersects with the national debate over institutional restructuring in the AI era and UI's own framing of AI as a workforce-readiness priority.
UI briefly paused new research activity during NIH administrative chaos in early 2025, affecting AI-adjacent biomedical research pipelines.

narrative_summary:
The University of Iowa enters 2026 with a notably deliberate AI posture for a Big Ten public R1. It has a published AI Strategy and Action Plan built on five guiding principles, a layered governance model (AI Steering Committee, AI SPARC, and a separate Digital Health and AI-Based Tool Governance committee at UI Health Care), and a clear set of implementation committees tied to its 2022-2027 Strategic Plan. Enterprise tooling is multi-vendor: Microsoft Copilot Chat is free to all ~31,500 students, faculty, and staff via HawkID SSO; Microsoft 365 Copilot is available as a $276/user/year paid tier; ChatGPT Edu was added December 2025; and an in-house Iowa AI Gateway is planned as a multi-model broker for 2026. Clinical AI is the most operationally mature surface: UI Health Care rolled out Evidently (AI chart review in Epic) and Nabla (ambient scribing) with a formal governance committee, and early data show 2,000+ clinician accesses on day one of Evidently and a 26% reduction in burnout symptoms from Nabla.

On the academic side, the Iowa Initiative for Artificial Intelligence (IIAI, founded 2018) remains the research center of gravity under Director Milan Sonka, operating pilot research funding, the HawkAI Level 1/2/3 proficiency certificates, and an AI/GPU Development Lab that feeds into the Argon HPC cluster (23,000+ CPU cores, 350+ GPUs). A new undergraduate Certificate in Artificial Intelligence launches fall 2026 with a deliberately cross-disciplinary curriculum (Computer Science, Philosophy, Art, Journalism, Languages, etc.), funded by a $195,000 P3 award. Tippie College of Business has one of the deeper business-school AI program suites in the Big Ten (graduate certificates, MBA concentration, Master's in Business Analytics).

Maturity dimensions where Iowa is weakest: there is no chief AI officer, no dedicated 'chief AI strategy' role outside IIAI, and AI-specific equity initiatives are still embedded inside general digital-accessibility compliance rather than standing on their own. Student-facing chatbots are still in pilot (HR, OneIT, Enrollment Management). Leadership transition is also in motion: longtime CIO Steve Fleagle retires spring 2026 and Brad Rohrer (University of Miami) begins as the next CIO in 2026 - his priorities will shape whether Iowa's careful, governance-first AI posture accelerates into a more ambitious 'AI-native' footing.

gaps_acknowledged: Total dollar spend on AI tooling and personnel is not disclosed publicly and can only be estimated from per-seat Copilot licensing ($276/user/year) and individual grants.
No public user-count figures for Copilot, Copilot Chat, or ChatGPT Edu at UI.
Identity of AI Steering Committee chair and full member roster is not public on the ITS site (the SPARC member roster is public but chair is not explicitly named).
No dedicated chief AI officer role exists; 'ai_strategy_lead' is inferred to be IIAI Director Milan Sonka based on public documentation.
No state/Regents-level AI policy specifically binding the three Regent universities was found; each campus has its own guidance.
Phase-two funding status of the NSF ImagiQ grant (post-phase-one) was not confirmed in this pass.
Controversies section is limited; no AI-specific lawsuits, FERPA incidents, or cheating-policy scandals were surfaced in the research window.
